items.title: Adelaide Festival appoints new Writers' Week director in wake of crisis

children.content: The new director of Adelaide Writers' Week says she is hopeful "really important lessons have been learned" following the implosion of this year's literary festival.

gallery.altText: A smiling woman leans an arm over a park bench

thumbnail.altText: A smiling woman leans an arm over a park bench

items.title: New grapevine virus GINV detected for first time in Australia

children.content: A grapevine virus known in China and Japan that affects plant growth and productivity has been detected in Australia for the first time in Victorian, Queensland and South Australian table grapes.

image.altText: Undergrowth of table grape vines.

items.title: Lack of 24-hour health care in SA prisons questioned

children.content: Advocates call for improved medical resourcing in South Australia's prisons after a coronial inquest into the death of an inmate.

image.altText: Sign at the entrance to low security prison Cadell Training Centre

items.title: Figure skating teen makes history for Australia

children.content: At just 15, Hana Bath became the first Australian female to ever land a triple axel in competition. The rising star now has her eye on the 2030 Winter Olympics.

gallery.altText: A figure skater wearing skates on ice holders her hands in the air

thumbnail.altText: A figure skater wearing skates on ice holders her hands in the air

items.title: 'Be honest with people': Politicians across the country react to NDIS cuts

children.content: State leaders are reacting to the sweeping cuts coming for the National Disability Insurance Scheme, with one minister arguing the federal government will "walk away" from those in need.

image.altText: Amanda Camm wearing a suit jacket and speaking into a microphone.

items.title: Woman faces murder charge over alleged hit-and-run that killed man

children.content: The accused's lawyer today told the court that her client was one of four young females in a car that was being attacked when she attempted to flee.

image.altText: A man puts an arm around a woman in front of neon lights and big glass windows

items.title: 'Unusual' warm weather sees Adelaide residents spending days at beach

children.content: Adelaide residents have been making the most of unusually warm weather for the end of April, with crowds flocking to the beach before the school holidays finish.

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer and white top

items.title: 'Very short on facts': Electoral Commission's bizarre communications in a crisis

children.content: As trust in their ability to effectively administer an election erodes by the day, so too seemingly does their transparency with the public.

image.altText: A woman holding a ballot walks past other people voting facing away in cardboard booths

items.title: Research for Congress raises doubts over AUKUS submarines

children.content: "Worrisome" construction delays plague the US Navy's submarine-building program, which Australia is relying on for its naval fleet.

image.altText: Several crew members walk on top of a submerged, docked submarine.

items.title: Stump that created hole in footpath becomes neighbourhood attraction

children.content: Adelaide residents have decorated a large tree stump and then the hole it left in a footpath to highlight the local council's delay in removing it.

image.altText: A rotten tree stump surrounded by traffic cones, including one topped by a green hat

items.title: Alleged drink driver detected at 151km/h with child in ute, police say

children.content: A man who was allegedly drink driving with a child inside a speeding ute in Adelaide's south last night will face court after being stripped of his licence, police say.

image.altText: An SA Police logo.

items.title: Woman's life 'changed for the better' after GPs in SA trained to diagnose ADHD

children.content: About 100 South Australian GPs are now qualified to diagnose ADHD and provide treatment, with the hope of that number doubling by the end of the year, with one patient describing her diagnosis through her GP as "life-changing".

image.altText: A person standing in the Adelaide CBD.

items.title: Childcare company CEO denies problems at centres a symptom of growing pains

children.content: The head of Edge Early Learning says he is conducting a "whole of business transformation project" after being the subject of compliance action over inadequate supervision and other issues at its childcare centres in Adelaide.

image.altText: A sign saying EDGE in front of a childcare centre

items.title: Farmers fear potential mouse plague as CSIRO warns of high numbers

children.content: As if the cost of diesel and the lack of fertiliser were not enough, South Australia's farmers are also facing the prospect of a mouse plague.

image.altText: a close up of a man's hands holding a live mouse

items.title: US watchdog announces Epstein files audit

children.content: The audit follows months of controversy over censorship, missed deadlines and the publication of survivors' details.

image.altText: ghislaine maxwell and jeffrey epstein seen together on a black background

items.title: Warnings of power price rises, missed renewable targets under proposed tax

children.content: The treasurer says changes to capital gains tax for foreign investors is a clarification, but experts say it could dampen investment.

articles.title: Albanese poised to kill off move to increase taxes on gas giants

children.content: The government will not increase taxes on gas giants in next month's budget, echoing the industry's argument that exporters are already paying tens of billions of dollars in taxes each year.

image.altText: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese seated for an interview in front of Australian, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander flags.

items.title: Charts explain why fertiliser prices aren't coming down anytime soon

children.content: Farmers around the world are going to be paying more for one of their most important inputs, fertiliser. Here are four charts that explain why.

image.altText: A hand holds brilliant white fertiliser while a truck tips it off in the background

items.title: Fuel filter: What to expect on Australian fuel supply and pricing for the weekend

children.content: The week's most important fuel news, analysis and pricing information.

image.altText: Fuel gauge graphic with dollar sign

items.title: Viva Energy boss says more refineries needed for secure fuel supply

children.content: The Viva Energy refinery provides about 10 per cent of Australia's fuel. Its chief, Scott Wyatt, chats to Alan Kohler about the company's future in the wake of the Geelong fire.

image.altText: An oil refinery

items.title: 'This is now terminal': UK braces for prospect of sixth PM in seven years

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer, who led Labour to a landslide general election victory in July 2024, has become so unpopular that even some colleagues are framing his demise as a case of not if, but when.

image.altText: A man in a suit looking down

items.title: 'Sad for us': Hannah Clarke's parents step back from advocacy

children.content: After their daughter and three grandchildren were murdered, Sue and Lloyd Clarke began a charity to improve awareness of coercive control. Now they've decided they need to step back for their own health.

image.altText: Hannah Clarke, hugs her son Trey Baxter, while standing on a beach.

items.title: Hundreds of fishing boats sit idle off southern China — but they're not there to fish

children.content: Off China's southern coast, hundreds of fishing boats sit idle. The crews aren't there to fish, but rather to "maintain presence" as part of a shadowy, state-funded fleet.

image.altText: A satellite image giving a birdseye view of boats scattered across a bay.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's eleventh-hour announcement, a disabled bird and a speedy humanoid robot

children.content: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

image.altText: A red and black robot on a street with barriers in the background and a ribbon falling to the ground.

items.title: The legacy of once-banned soldiers that hides in plain sight

children.content: Thousands of Indian soldiers served alongside Anzac forces at Gallipoli, leaving their legacy in the "puggaree" — a traditional Indian headwrap that was adapted to adorn the slouch hats worn by the Australian army.

image.altText: Six men with red and white striped turbans in khaki uniforms with red and white around their waist and long white socks.

items.title: Jane Fonda was in her 40s when she changed the way we exercise

children.content: In the 1980s, the fitness industry as we know it was just getting started. One of the most important people in developing the way we exercise today was Jane Fonda, who released a best-selling aerobics video in 1982.

gallery.altText: Actress Jane Fonda in a yellow exercise outfit stretching with her left foot pointed upward in the air.

thumbnail.altText: Actress Jane Fonda in a yellow exercise outfit stretching with her left foot pointed upward in the air.

items.title: Which states get a public holiday for Anzac Day?

children.content: Anzac Day is observed on April 25 every year. This year that date falls on a Saturday, but that doesn't mean a long weekend for everyone.

image.altText: poppies on a cenotaph

items.title: Documents reveal fresh details about Roberts-Smith's overseas moving plans

children.content: Police were concerned war veteran Ben Roberts-Smith was attempting to move overseas and possibly to Singapore or Spain, court documents reveal.

image.altText: Ben Roberts-Smith looks at the camera outside a court building

items.title: Genesis Owusu goes out of his way to connect with fans — and detractors

children.content: Genesis Owusu today releases his new track Life Keeps Going from his upcoming third album Redstar Wu & the Worldwide Scourge, as he encourages engagement with people who don't agree with his political views.

image.altText: Genesis Owusu bare-chested on stage singing into a microphone, yellow background, Kente cloth from waist down

items.title: Bulldogs offer update after star's horror fall as Curnow kicks a bag

children.content: Sydney's hold on top spot is strengthened by a 66-point win over the Western Bulldogs, whose injury woes continue.

image.altText: nauigthon

items.title: Sheraz risked his life translating in Afghanistan. His family paid the price

children.content: Sheraz worked with Australian and US troops in Afghanistan, as a result his family were targeted by the Taliban. This Anzac Day he's being heroed.

image.altText: A man seated at a table, smiling.

items.title: Award-winning author who put Brisbane on the literary map dies aged 92

children.content: Erudite and urbane, David Malouf — perhaps most famous for his portrait of mid-century Brisbane in his 1975 novel Johnno — will be fondly remembered for his immeasurable contribution to Australian letters.

image.altText: Author David Malouf.

items.title: Hackers steal $3.7m from Sri Lanka earmarked for debts to Australia

children.content: Cyber criminals have hacked Sri Lanka's finance ministry, stealing millions of dollars destined to repay Australia.

image.altText: Cyberattack illustration

items.title: Russell Brand admits to sex with 16yo when he was 30

children.content: British actor and comedian Russell Brand, who is facing multiple sexual assault charges, has admitted to having sex with a 16-year-old when he was 30.

image.altText: A man dressing black with a pair of sunglasses on

items.title: Ancient fossils reveal when the iconic Twelve Apostles formed

children.content: An analysis of tiny fossils of sea creatures found in rock samples around the Port Campbell region in south-western Victoria has refined our understanding of how the Twelve Apostles formed.

Large limestone monoliths and coastal cliffs silhouetted in red afternoon glow.
